source.language_note: Rulebook Chinese; values restated in SI.
source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from a CUADC 2025 methanol-engine logistics UAV wing structure (main spar + rear spar + compression/tension ribs + root joint fitting + tip cap + aileron hinges + skin panels). No rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the main wing structural assembly for a CUADC 2025 methanol-engine logistics UAV (6.5 cc methanol, MTOW ~12 kg, cruise 20 m/s, span 2.4 m) as a multi-part bolted/bonded assembly rather than a single-piece spar. The assembly consists of: the main pultruded CFRP rectangular-section main spar (40 x 20 mm external, 3 mm wall cap, full 2400 mm span through a 100 mm fuselage carry-through with 4 x M6 bolts in an 80 x 30 mm pattern); a rear spar running parallel to the main spar at the rear of the torsion box; a set of compression ribs at rib stations; tension ribs near the payload station; a root-joint fuselage fitting; a wingtip cap; aileron hinge brackets; and skin panels bonded to the spars and ribs. Ribs attach at 8 stations spaced 300 mm apart; payload drop bay imposes a 2 kg point load at station 3 from the root (900 mm outboard) on each wing. Material is pultruded CFRP (Ex = 130 GPa, Ey = 9 GPa, Gxy = 5 GPa, nu_xy = 0.3, density 1500 kg/m^3, tensile allowable 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa, ILSS 45 MPa). Load cases: LC1 1 g cruise 60 N per wing lift (elliptical); LC2 3 g pull-up 180 N per wing; LC3 payload-drop transient 20 g on 2 kg payload at the 900 mm station; LC4 aerodynamic torsion 4 Nm/m distributed; LC5 tip ground-handling 50 N at wingtip. Pass/fail: (R1) LC1/LC2/LC4/LC5 max composite stress <= 1000 MPa; (R2) LC3 peak dynamic stress <= 1250 MPa; (R3) LC2 tip deflection <= 80 mm; (R4) LC2 first-mode buckling >= 4.0; (R5) spar mass <= 450 g; (R6) LC4 tip twist <= 2 deg. Primary verification: linear static FEA (LC1, LC2, LC4, LC5), linear buckling (LC2), transient (LC3). Vibration modal, thermal, fluid, radiation not required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 assembly + report.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
The wing is decomposed into: (A) one main pultruded CFRP rectangular-section main spar (40 x 20 mm external, 2400 mm span); (B) one rear spar (secondary, smaller section, 2400 mm span) at the rear of the torsion box; (C) 10 compression ribs at ~240 mm pitch along each half-span (CFRP laminate, 2 mm thick); (D) 2 tension ribs at the payload station (laminate with metal reinforcement); (E) one root-joint fuselage fitting (aluminium lug carrying the 4x M6 carry-through bolts and transferring moments into the fuselage); (F) two wingtip caps (one per tip); (G) 4 aileron hinge brackets on the rear spar (2 per aileron, both wings); (H) upper and lower skin panels (CFRP laminate, bonded to spars and ribs forming the D-box torsion cell); (I) M6/M3 fastener set. Interfaces: IF1 main-spar to rear-spar joined through compression ribs; IF2 ribs bonded + bolted to main spar; IF3 ribs bonded + bolted to rear spar; IF4 skin panels bonded to spar caps and ribs (D-box); IF5 root fitting bolted (4x M6) to main-spar web and to fuselage; IF6 aileron hinge brackets bolted to rear spar; IF7 wingtip caps bonded to both spars at tips. FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED multi-body model only — per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection DCR must be <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC2 3 g pull-up). All inherited load magnitudes, material properties, and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design the main wing structural assembly for a CUADC 2025 methanol-engine logistics UAV (6.5 cc methanol, MTOW ~12 kg, cruise 20 m/s, span 2.4 m) as a multi-part bolted/bonded assembly.

Envelope: main pultruded CFRP rectangular-section spar 40 x 20 mm external, 3 mm wall cap, full 2400 mm span through a 100 mm fuselage carry-through with 4x M6 bolts on 80 x 30 mm pattern. Ribs at 8 stations spaced 300 mm; 2 kg payload point load at station 3 (900 mm outboard).

Material: pultruded CFRP (tensile allowable 1500 MPa, compressive 900 MPa, ILSS 45 MPa).

Load cases: LC1 1 g cruise 60 N per wing; LC2 3 g pull-up 180 N per wing; LC3 payload-drop transient 20 g on 2 kg payload at 900 mm station; LC4 aerodynamic torsion 4 Nm/m distributed; LC5 tip ground-handling 50 N at wingtip.

Pass/fail: (R1) LC1/LC2/LC4/LC5 composite stress <= 1000 MPa; (R2) LC3 peak dynamic <= 1250 MPa; (R3) LC2 tip deflection <= 80 mm; (R4) LC2 buckling eigenvalue >= 4.0; (R5) spar mass <= 450 g; (R6) LC4 tip twist <= 2 deg.

prompt.title: CUADC 2025 methanol logistics UAV wing (multi-part spar/rib/skin assembly)
prompt.brief: Pultruded CFRP wing structure: main spar + rear spar + 10 compression ribs per half-span + 2 tension ribs + root fitting + 2 wingtip caps + 4 aileron hinge brackets + bonded D-box skin panels. FEM verification at assembled level only; connection DCR <= 1.0 under LC2 governing 3 g pull-up.

boundary_conditions.mounting: root fitting fully fixed (4x M6 carry-through); tip free
boundary_conditions.loads: elliptical lift distribution on each half-span, payload point load at 900 mm station, aerodynamic pitching torsion, or tip handling force

parts.name: main_spar
parts.role: pultruded CFRP rectangular-section main spar carrying wing bending (full 2400 mm span)

parts.name: rear_spar
parts.role: secondary spar at rear of torsion box carrying trailing-edge loads and aileron hinges

parts.name: compression_rib
parts.role: CFRP laminate compression rib at ~240 mm pitch along each half-span

parts.name: tension_rib
parts.role: tension rib at the 900 mm payload station carrying the 2 kg payload point load and LC3 transient

parts.name: root_joint_fitting
parts.role: aluminium root-joint fuselage fitting carrying the 4x M6 carry-through bolts and transferring moments into fuselage

parts.name: aileron_hinge_bracket
parts.role: aileron hinge bracket on rear spar (2 per aileron, both wings)

parts.name: skin_panel
parts.role: upper and lower CFRP skin panels bonded to spar caps and ribs forming the D-box torsion cell

parts.name: fastener_set
parts.role: M6 carry-through bolts + M3 rib/hinge bolts + structural adhesive

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 2400 mm span x ~260 mm chord x ~40 mm thickness
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: +Y spanwise (root to tip), +X chord (leading to trailing), +Z up

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all inter-part interfaces <= 1.0 under the governing load case (LC2 3 g pull-up).

interfaces.load_path: aerodynamic + payload load from ribs into main-spar caps
trailing-edge load from ribs into rear spar
LC3 payload-drop 20 g shock from tension rib into main spar
D-box torsion shear flow from skin into spar caps
wing-root bending moment + shear into fuselage root fitting
aileron hinge moments into rear spar
tip closure for torsion cell continuity

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. The LC2 wing-root bending crosses rib-to-spar bonds, skin bonds, and the 4x M6 root fitting; assembly-level DCR is required, per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A CFRP logistics UAV wing of this class is built from main + rear spar + discrete ribs + bonded D-box skin + aluminium root fitting + wingtip closures + aileron hinge brackets, not a single monolithic spar; the bending + torsion + payload-drop transient loads flow across bonded and bolted joints that must be modelled as a multi-body assembly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Rib count (~10 compression + 2 tension per wing), skin panel count (2 per wing), and aileron-hinge count (4) are programmatic speculation consistent with the rib-spacing and span constraints; spar geometry, loads, material, and pass/fail thresholds inherit the original brief verbatim.
